recipes.title: Muhammara
recipes.description: Aleppine dip of charred red peppers, walnuts and pomegranate molasses, smoky-sweet with a fiery kick of Aleppo pepper.
steps: Roast the peppers under a hot grill for 15 minutes, turning until blackened all over.
Steam them in a covered bowl for 10 minutes, then peel and seed.
Toast the walnuts in a dry pan until fragrant.
Pulse peppers, walnuts, breadcrumbs, garlic, molasses, spices and salt to a coarse paste.
Stream in olive oil and lemon juice while pulsing to a thick spread.
Spread on a plate, drizzle with extra olive oil and serve with warm flatbread.

recipes.title: Yalanji
recipes.description: Cold meatless stuffed vine leaves filled with lemony rice, herbs and pomegranate molasses, eaten as a mezze.
steps: Soak rinsed rice for 20 minutes, then drain.
Mix rice with chopped tomato, herbs, spring onions, half the oil, half the molasses, sumac and salt.
Lay each leaf vein-side up, place a heaped teaspoon of filling and roll tightly.
Line a heavy pot with extra leaves and pack the rolls in tight layers.
Combine remaining oil, molasses, lemon juice and water and pour over.
Weight with a plate, cover and simmer 45 minutes; cool to room temperature before serving.

recipes.title: Kibbeh Bil Sanieh
recipes.description: Aleppo's tray-baked kibbeh with a layer of spiced minced lamb and pine nuts sandwiched between bulgur dough.
steps: Soak bulgur 20 minutes and squeeze dry, then process with lean lamb, one onion, salt and 1 tsp allspice to a paste.
Brown the second chopped onion in ghee, add filling lamb, pine nuts and remaining spices and cook 8 minutes.
Press half the kibbeh dough firmly into a greased tray as an even layer.
Spread the cooled filling evenly across the base.
Top with remaining kibbeh, smoothed with wet hands; score into diamonds and brush with olive oil.
Bake at 200°C for 35 minutes until deep golden, then rest 10 minutes before cutting.

recipes.title: Shish Barak
recipes.description: Tiny dumplings of spiced lamb baked until golden, then floated in a warm garlicky yogurt sauce.
steps: Knead flour, water and 1 tsp salt to a smooth dough; rest 30 minutes.
Brown the lamb with chopped onion, allspice and salt for 6 minutes and cool.
Roll the dough thin, cut into 4 cm rounds, fill with lamb and pinch into tortellini shapes.
Bake the dumplings at 200°C for 12 minutes until pale gold.
Whisk yogurt with the egg and cornstarch in a heavy pot and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thickened.
Slip the dumplings into the yogurt; finish with garlic and coriander fried briefly in ghee.

recipes.title: Fattet Hummus
recipes.description: Damascene breakfast bowl of toasted pita, warm chickpeas and garlicky tahini-yogurt sauce, finished with paprika butter.
steps: Tear pita and toast at 200°C for 6 minutes until crisp.
Warm chickpeas in their cooking liquid with salt.
Whisk yogurt with tahini, crushed garlic, lemon and salt.
Layer pita in a deep dish, top with drained chickpeas, then yogurt sauce.
Toast pine nuts in ghee, stir in paprika and pour over the dish while sizzling.
Scatter parsley and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Sheikh El Mahshi
recipes.description: Aubergines stuffed with spiced minced lamb and pine nuts, simmered in a rich tomato sauce until silky.
steps: Slit each aubergine lengthwise without cutting through and salt for 20 minutes.
Pat dry and shallow-fry the aubergines in oil until tender; drain on paper.
Brown the lamb with chopped onion, pine nuts, allspice, cinnamon and salt.
Stuff each aubergine generously with the lamb mixture.
Lay them in a wide pot, whisk tomato paste with water and salt and pour around.
Cover and simmer 35 minutes until the sauce thickens; serve with rice.

recipes.title: Mahshi Malfouf
recipes.description: Cabbage leaves wrapped tightly around lemony rice and lamb, simmered with garlic and dried mint until fragrant.
steps: Core the cabbage and blanch the whole head in boiling water, peeling off leaves as they soften.
Cut large leaves in half along the spine and trim thick ribs.
Mix rinsed rice with lamb, allspice, cinnamon, salt and a tablespoon of olive oil.
Place a finger of filling on each leaf and roll tightly into thin cigars.
Layer rolls in a heavy pot scattered with peeled garlic cloves; weight with a plate.
Pour water, lemon juice, dried mint and remaining oil over and simmer covered for 50 minutes.

recipes.title: Kebab Halabi
recipes.description: Aleppine kebabs of minced lamb baked over sliced tomato and Aleppo pepper sauce, sharp with pomegranate and chili.
steps: Grate the onion and squeeze dry, then mix with lamb, chopped parsley, allspice, half the Aleppo pepper and salt.
Knead the meat for 5 minutes until sticky and shape into 12 fat fingers.
Slice tomatoes and arrange them across the base of an oven dish.
Lay the kebabs over the tomatoes; whisk tomato paste, molasses, oil, remaining Aleppo pepper and 100 ml water and pour over.
Bake at 220°C for 25 minutes until the kebabs are cooked and the sauce is bubbling.
Rest 5 minutes and serve with rice or flatbread.

recipes.title: Mahshi Filfil
recipes.description: Sweet bell peppers stuffed with rice, minced meat and tomato, simmered in a light tomato broth until tender.
steps: Slice the tops off the peppers and remove the seeds, keeping the caps as lids.
Mix rinsed rice with lamb, diced tomato, 1 tablespoon tomato paste, spices and salt.
Stuff each pepper two-thirds full and replace the cap.
Stand them upright in a heavy pot.
Whisk remaining tomato paste, olive oil and water and pour around the peppers.
Cover and simmer 50 minutes until the rice is fluffy and the peppers are tender.

recipes.title: Fattoush bil Rumman
recipes.description: Damascene autumn fattoush dressed with pomegranate molasses and fresh seeds, vibrant with sumac and herbs.
steps: Tear the pita, brush with olive oil and toast at 200°C for 6 minutes until crisp.
Chop lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, radishes and spring onions to bite size.
Tear in the mint leaves and combine in a wide bowl.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, molasses, sumac and salt together.
Toss the salad with the dressing and pita chips just before serving.
Scatter the fresh pomegranate seeds generously over the top.

recipes.title: Awameh
recipes.description: Crisp fried dough puffs soaked in fragrant sugar syrup with lemon and orange blossom, eaten throughout the Levant.
steps: Whisk flour, cornstarch, yeast, sugar, salt and warm water to a thick batter; let rise 1 hour.
Make syrup by simmering sugar, water and lemon juice for 10 minutes, then add blossom water and cool.
Heat oil to 170°C and drop teaspoonfuls of batter into the oil with a wet spoon.
Fry for 4-5 minutes, turning often, until deep golden and crisp.
Drain briefly, then drop into the cool syrup for 1 minute.
Lift onto a plate and serve while still warm and crisp.

recipes.title: Mhalabieh
recipes.description: Silky milk pudding set with cornstarch and rose water, topped with chopped pistachios and crushed dried rose.
steps: Whisk cornstarch into 200 ml of cold milk until completely smooth.
Heat the rest of the milk with sugar in a heavy pot until just steaming.
Pour the cornstarch slurry into the warm milk, whisking constantly.
Cook over medium heat 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thickened to a custard.
Stir in rose and orange blossom waters, then divide between cups.
Chill 3 hours and top with pistachios and rose petals before serving.
